name: San Francisco Chops
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Dry sherry or broth (OR dry white wine)
3 tablespoons Soy sauce (I used ginger-flavored soy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the fat from the pork chops or ribs.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a skillet and brown the meat on both sides.
3. In a bowl, mix together garlic, 2 teaspoons oil, sherry or broth, soy sauce, brown sugar, and crushed red pepper.
4. Pour the mixture over the meat and cover the pan tightly.
5. Simmer for 30-35 minutes, adding 1-2 tablespoons water if needed to prevent drying out.
6. Dissolve cornstarch in a little water and stir into the pan; cook until thickened.
7. Serve the pork chops or ribs over parsley buttered noodles.
8. Garnish each portion with chopped green onions and peeled, sliced carrots before serving.
